gazette: the Directorate General of Trade Research and Risk Assessment
title: Canada Border Services Agency's (CBSA) announces its new external launch date for CARM
bodyHtml:
According to the announcement made by the Directorate General of Trade Research and Risk Assessment:

With the notification dated May 22, 2024, by Canada's customs authority, the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA), the Canadian Government has changed the planned implementation date of the "CARM" digital system to October 21, 2024. Accordingly, the "CARM" system will officially be operational as of October 21, 2024, for the collection of customs duties and other taxes on imports into Canada, and will be accessible to all relevant stakeholders in the trade chain.

Thus, the "CARM" digital system will become the official record system of the CBSA as of October 21, 2024, and will be the official platform that Canadian importers and other partners in the trade chain will use to pay their owed customs duties and other taxes.

In this context, firms with "non-resident importer" status to Canada, customs brokers, and commercial service providers should complete their registration to the "CARM" system before October 21, 2024. Exporters should closely follow the process in consultation with their partner importers or customs brokers to ensure that export transactions to Canada are carried out smoothly.

All businesses importing goods to Canada, whether based in Canada or not, and other partners in the trade chain dealing with the CBSA must register on the "CARM" platform. In this framework, it is essential for Canadian importers and businesses with the status of "Non-Resident Importer (NRI)" who send goods to their Canadian customers and are responsible for customs clearance and other import-related procedures to register on the "CARM Client Portal" and appoint a trade account manager through "CARM."

Information regarding the registration and processes for Canadian importers, customs clearance companies, trade consultancy service providers, and financial guarantee service providers to the "CARM" platform can be accessed via the following link:

https://www.cbsa-asfc.gc.ca/services/carm-gcra/start-passer-eng.html#s1

The CBSA's notification also states that "CARM" was made available for the CBSA's internal corporate use as of May 13, 2024, and that harmonization and implementation efforts for the transition to the digital system are progressing successfully.

The notification further notes that stakeholders in the trade chain continue to register on the "CARM" digital system, with over 81,000 registrations completed to date; 95% of Canada's largest importers by trade value have registered on the system. Relevant stakeholders can register on the platform via the following link:

https://ccp-pcc.cbsa-asfc.cloud-nuage.canada.ca/en/auth/login
